title: Lodh Falls
tagline: Lodh Falls — heritage city of Jharkhand

overview.short: The Lodh Falls (also known as Budha Ghagh) is a waterfall on the Burha River in the mid-forest of Palamau Tiger Reserve, in Latehar district of Jharkhand state in India.
overview.description: The Lodh Falls (also known as Budha Ghagh) is a waterfall on the Burha River in the mid-forest of Palamau Tiger Reserve, in Latehar district of Jharkhand state in India. It is the highest waterfall in Jharkhand and the 21st highest waterfall in India.

topPlaces.name: Mahuadanr Wolf Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Mahuadanr Wolf Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Latehar district of Jharkhand. It is part of Palamau Tiger Reserve.

items.note: Mahuadanr Wolf Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary located in Latehar district of Jharkhand. It is part of Palamau Tiger Reserve.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lodh Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lodh Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lodh Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Birsa Munda Airport, Ranchi (134 km) and the nearest railway station is Ranchi Junction (134 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lodh Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lodh Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lodh Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Lodh Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lodh Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mahuadanr Wolf Sanctuary.
